title: Keyworth Leisure Centre refurbishment and energy-efficiency upgrades (reopened September 2025)
description: Rushcliffe Borough Council completed a refurbishment of Keyworth Leisure Centre, including upgrades intended to improve the building and reduce energy use, and reopened the facility to the public in September 2025.
statusRationale: Council announcement confirms refurbishment completed and centre reopened on 16 September 2025.
locationSource: Project description and Rushcliffe Borough Council news announcement referencing 'Keyworth Leisure Centre' reopening after refurbishment (https://www.rushcliffe.gov.uk/news-area/keyworth-leisure-centre-refurbishment-unveiled-as-it-reopens/)

evidence.title: Keyworth Leisure Centre refurbishment unveiled as it reopens
evidence.summary: Council update announcing the reopening of Keyworth Leisure Centre following refurbishment and energy-efficiency upgrades, with works described and reopening confirmed.

title: Sheerwater: refurbishment of long-term empty homes funded via Local Authority Housing Fund
description: Woking Borough Council secured funding to refurbish long-term empty homes in the Sheerwater area for use as temporary accommodation, as part of council-led interventions connected to the Sheerwater regeneration context.
statusRationale: Council announcement confirms funding secured with refurbishments planned to complete by March 2026.
locationDescription: Sheerwater suburb/area of Woking, Surrey (representative centroid for the Sheerwater neighbourhood where empty homes are being refurbished)
locationSource: Project description and Woking Borough Council news release referencing Sheerwater, Woking (no specific property addresses provided)

evidence.title: Grant funding secured to refurbish empty homes in Sheerwater
evidence.summary: Council news release describing the award of Local Authority Housing Fund grant funding to refurbish long-term empty homes in Sheerwater for temporary accommodation, with an expected completion timeframe.

title: Crawley Station Gateway: phase one public realm and bus station operational improvements (planning permission granted)
description: Crawley Borough Council granted planning permission for phase one of the Station Gateway regeneration scheme, covering public realm improvements on key routes between the railway station and bus station and operational improvements at the bus station.
statusRationale: Council news confirms phase one planning permission granted and describes the funded scheme scope.
locationDescription: Crawley Station Gateway area around Crawley Railway Station and Crawley Bus Station (Friary Way/The Martlets/Haslett Avenue West), Crawley town centre, West Sussex
locationSource: Crawley Borough Council news release (2025-02-17) describing Phase 1 works on routes between Crawley railway station and bus station and naming Friary Way, The Martlets and Haslett Avenue West

evidence.title: Planning permission granted for first phase of Station Gateway regeneration scheme
evidence.summary: Council announcement confirming planning permission for phase one of Station Gateway, describing public space improvements and bus station operational changes and identifying the streets covered.

title: Portland Square (Sutton in Ashfield) public realm transformation works
description: Ashfield District Council began on-site works to transform Portland Square in Sutton in Ashfield, including new raised lawns, seating, semi-mature trees, upgraded lighting, new paving and CCTV, as part of its town centre transformation programme.
statusRationale: Council news confirms ground was broken at Portland Square and indicates mid-2025 expected completion.
locationDescription: Portland Square, Sutton-in-Ashfield town centre, Nottinghamshire
locationSource: Ashfield District Council news release (2024-10-21) stating works are at Portland Square, Sutton in Ashfield

evidence.title: Excitement builds as work starts on flagship stage of Sutton transformation
evidence.summary: Council update announcing the start of works at Portland Square and summarising planned public realm improvements, with completion expected mid-2025.

title: Coopers Hill, Bracknell: regeneration and public realm works completed
description: Bracknell Forest Council confirmed completion of regeneration works at Coopers Hill in Bracknell, including improvements to the local environment and public realm.
statusRationale: Council news release states the Coopers Hill regeneration has been completed.
locationDescription: Coopers Hill, Bracknell, Bracknell Forest, Berkshire

evidence.title: Coopers Hill regeneration completed
evidence.summary: Council media release confirming completion of regeneration works at Coopers Hill in Bracknell.

title: Andover town centre riverside park transformation (planning permission granted)
description: Test Valley Borough Council approved planning permission to transform a riverside park in Andover town centre, with proposals to improve green space and accessibility as part of a wider town centre programme.
statusRationale: Council update confirms planning permission granted and indicates next-stage design and delivery activity.
locationDescription: Riverside Park, Andover town centre, Test Valley Borough, Hampshire
locationSource: Test Valley Borough Council project update page for Andover Town Centre Project – Riverside Park (planning permission granted)

evidence.title: Andover Town Centre Project: Riverside Park Update - Planning Permission Granted
evidence.summary: Council project page update stating planning permission was granted to transform the riverside park in Andover town centre and outlining proposed improvements.

title: Ramsgate Green Campus: planning application published for education and regeneration scheme
description: Thanet District Council published a planning application for the Green Campus scheme in Ramsgate, setting out a proposal intended to support skills, education and regeneration objectives within the town.
statusRationale: Council project page states the planning application was published and notes the expected decision timeline.
locationDescription: Ramsgate town centre, Thanet District, Kent (representative point for the Green Campus scheme in Ramsgate; specific site not identified in provided evidence).
locationSource: Thanet District Council Green Campus project page (https://www.thanet.gov.uk/green-campus/) indicating the scheme is in Ramsgate, Thanet.

evidence.title: Green Campus
evidence.summary: Council project page stating that the Green Campus planning application was published on 15 September 2025 and outlining the expected planning decision timeframe.

title: Staveley 21: town centre public realm and access improvements programme
description: Chesterfield Borough Council’s Staveley 21 programme sets out a package of town centre improvements in Staveley, including public realm, accessibility and place-making works funded through the Staveley Towns Fund programme.
statusRationale: Council project page confirms the Staveley 21 programme scope and funding context under the Towns Fund.
locationDescription: Staveley town centre, Staveley, Chesterfield, Derbyshire
locationSource: Chesterfield Borough Council – Staveley 21 Towns Fund project page (town centre improvements in Staveley)

evidence.title: Staveley 21
evidence.summary: Council project page describing the Staveley 21 Towns Fund programme and its intended public realm and accessibility improvements in Staveley town centre, with a page update date shown as 10 December 2024.

title: Cirencester Leisure Centre refurbishment and decarbonisation works unveiled
description: Cotswold District Council completed refurbishment and decarbonisation works at Cirencester Leisure Centre and unveiled the upgrades in September 2024.
statusRationale: Council press release states refurbishment was completed and unveiled on 30 September 2024.
locationDescription: Cirencester Leisure Centre, Cirencester, Gloucestershire (Cotswold District)
locationSource: Cotswold District Council press release (30 Sep 2024) referencing refurbishment and decarbonisation works at Cirencester Leisure Centre

title: Carlisle city centre regeneration: Market Square, Greenmarket and Devonshire Street works update
description: Cumberland Council published a status update on multiple Carlisle city centre regeneration works, covering progress at Market Square, Greenmarket and Devonshire Street and related town centre improvements.
statusRationale: Council update describes a programme with elements completed and others underway during September 2024.
locationDescription: Carlisle city centre (centroid near Market Square/Greenmarket/Devonshire Street, Carlisle, Cumbria)

evidence.summary: Council update describing progress across Carlisle city centre regeneration sites including Market Square, Greenmarket and Devonshire Street, with status notes on works completed and in progress.

title: Burton town centre cycleway extension (works starting January 2026)
description: Staffordshire County Council announced works to extend the Burton town centre cycleway, with construction scheduled to begin in January 2026 as part of the broader Burton town centre programme.
statusRationale: County council newsroom item confirms scheme scope and a January 2026 start date for works.
locationDescription: Burton upon Trent town centre, Staffordshire (representative point for the town centre cycleway extension works area)
locationSource: Staffordshire County Council newsroom article (2025-12-16) 'Work to extend Burton town centre cycleway begins next month' referencing 'Burton town centre cycleway'

evidence.title: Work to extend Burton town centre cycleway begins next month
evidence.summary: County council newsroom article announcing works to extend Burton town centre cycleway, with works due to begin in January 2026.

title: Otterpool Park: wastewater treatment works planning application validated
description: Folkestone & Hythe District Council validated a planning application for a wastewater treatment works associated with the Otterpool Park development, marking a key utilities infrastructure step for the new settlement proposals.
statusRationale: Council news release confirms the wastewater treatment works planning application was validated and sets out an indicative determination timeline.
locationDescription: Otterpool Park development area (near Westenhanger/Otterpool, Folkestone & Hythe District, Kent)
locationSource: Project description and Folkestone & Hythe District Council news release referencing the Otterpool Park development area (wastewater treatment works application validated)

evidence.title: Otterpool Park Wastewater Treatment Works planning application validated
evidence.summary: Council announcement confirming the planning application for the Otterpool Park wastewater treatment works was validated and describing the next steps and indicative determination timescales.

title: Cornhill Market, Lincoln: refurbished market hall reopens for traders
description: A refurbished Cornhill Market in Lincoln city centre reopened for traders and new businesses, reflecting a city-centre regeneration investment in a key civic and commercial asset.
statusRationale: External community report references traders opening in the refurbished Cornhill Market in May 2024.
locationDescription: Cornhill Market (market hall), Lincoln city centre, Lincoln, Lincolnshire
locationSource: Project description and Lincoln City Foundation article referencing the refurbished Cornhill Market in Lincoln city centre

evidence.title: ESOL learner opens new business in refurbished Cornhill Market
evidence.summary: Article describing a trader opening a business in the refurbished Cornhill Market and referencing the market’s reopening period in May 2024.

title: Newcastle-under-Lyme: regeneration sites and schemes approved to progress (contracts programme)
description: Newcastle-under-Lyme Borough Council reported that a package of regeneration sites and schemes across the borough had been approved to progress through procurement and delivery steps, supported by contract approvals and programme funding.
statusRationale: Council news release states regeneration sites are ready to progress and references contract approvals and next steps.
locationDescription: Newcastle-under-Lyme town centre (representative point for the multi-site regeneration programme across Newcastle-under-Lyme Borough, centred on Newcastle town centre), Staffordshire, England
locationSource: Project description and Newcastle-under-Lyme Borough Council news release indicating multiple regeneration sites including Newcastle town centre

evidence.title: £85 million of regeneration sites ready to progress
evidence.summary: Council announcement outlining a regeneration programme and stating that sites are ready to progress, referencing the approval of contracts and programme delivery steps.

title: St Mary’s Gate, Rochdale: new pedestrian crossing completed
description: Rochdale Borough Council completed a new pedestrian crossing on St Mary’s Gate in Rochdale town centre, intended to improve pedestrian connectivity as part of wider public realm and town centre improvements.
statusRationale: Council news confirms the crossing is complete and open.
locationDescription: St Mary’s Gate, Rochdale town centre (representative point on St Mary’s Gate near central Rochdale)
locationSource: Rochdale Borough Council news release: https://www.rochdale.gov.uk/news/article/1158/st-marys-gate-crossing-complete (states crossing on St Mary’s Gate in Rochdale town centre, but does not specify an exact junction)

evidence.title: St Mary’s Gate crossing complete
evidence.summary: Council announcement stating the new pedestrian crossing on St Mary’s Gate in Rochdale is complete, improving access and connectivity.

title: Bridgwater Tidal Barrier: arrival of jack-up barge milestone for construction
description: A GOV.UK Environment Agency press release confirmed the arrival of a jack-up barge at the Bridgwater Tidal Barrier site, a milestone enabling the next phase of construction for the major flood defence scheme on the River Parrett.
statusRationale: Environment Agency press release confirms active construction milestone reached in March 2025.
locationDescription: Bridgwater Tidal Barrier construction site on the River Parrett, Bridgwater, Somerset

evidence.title: Bridgwater Tidal Barrier sees arrival of giant construction barge
evidence.summary: Environment Agency press release describing the arrival of a jack-up barge to support cofferdam and foundation works at the Bridgwater Tidal Barrier site, confirming a construction milestone.

title: Paignton and Preston Promenade: coastal flood defences and public realm improvements (developer appointed)
description: Torbay Council appointed a developer to deliver improved coastal flood defences and wider public space upgrades along Paignton and Preston Promenade, intended to strengthen resilience to storm damage and flooding while improving the seafront environment.
statusRationale: Council press release confirms developer appointment and indicates the start of works from September 2025.
locationDescription: Paignton & Preston Promenade seafront (between Paignton Sands and Preston Sands), Torbay, Devon

evidence.title: Developer appointed for improved flood defences at Paignton and Preston Promenade
evidence.summary: Council press release announcing the appointment of a developer to deliver improved coastal flood defences and public space upgrades along Paignton and Preston Promenade, with works scheduled to begin from September 2025.

title: Amber Valley play area refurbishment programme (contract award)
description: Amber Valley Borough Council awarded a contract for play area refurbishment works across the district, covering improvements to playground and play equipment assets as part of a planned works programme.
statusRationale: Contracts Finder notice indicates the contract award and a defined contract period for refurbishment works.
locationDescription: Amber Valley district, Derbyshire (district-wide play area refurbishment programme; representative centroid near Ripley)
locationSource: Contracts Finder contract award notice (district-wide Amber Valley Borough Council programme; no single named site)
